_doc: IN-side adapters for the diversity atlas. An extractor takes a dict of raw output paths (as returned by the runner) plus a small params dict, and returns a layer payload — a Python dict that validates against schemas/schema_out/<schema_version>.schema.json. Mirrors atlases/inversion/registries/data/extractors.registry.json. Parsers under atlases/diversity/registries/extractors/ are Python modules; the dispatcher imports them per-call via importlib. _stage values: 'staging' = loose passthrough, schema is permissive; 'normalized' = typed payload that validates against a strict schema.

extractors._doc: Existing extractor — parses a captured /api/diversity/{slot} JSON payload verbatim under payload.body. Paired with the import_slot action.

Parses 04_summary/genomewide_heterozygosity.tsv into a typed per-sample table. Emits { samples: [{ sample_id, het_genomewide, n_sites_sfs, sfs_bin0, sfs_bin1 }], summary: { n_samples, median_het, mean_het, ... } }.
Parses a per-sample {sample}.est.ml file into { sample_id, bins: [bin0, bin1], folded: true, h: bin1 / (bin0 + bin1) }. realSFS -fold 1 emits two whitespace-separated values.

Parses an ANGSD doThetaStat {sample}.win{N}.step{M}.pestPG file. Skips the header annotation row. Emits { sample_id, win_bp, step_bp, windows: [{ chrom, win_center, tW, tP, tajima, n_sites }], summary: { n_windows, mean_pi, median_pi, max_tajima, min_tajima } }.

Generic TSV-to-rows passthrough used by the ancestry_het_* per-K layers and any other tabular file whose shape doesn't justify a dedicated extractor. Emits { columns: [...], rows: [{...}, ...], row_count, _provenance: { source_path, parsed_at } }. Numeric columns coerced when infer_types is true; '-nan' / '-inf' / 'NA' all become null.

Specialised wrapper over the generic TSV extractor for kruskal_K{K}_{cohort}.tsv: normalizes dot-suffixed R column names so the JS consumer doesn't have to. Emits the same shape as tsv_table_v1 but advertised as a normalized stage.

Planned extractor for per-sample ROH segment tables. Parser module not yet written — see MISSING_DATA.md §2 (ROH pipeline output is empty on disk today). Emits { sample_id, segments: [{ chrom, start_bp, end_bp, length_bp, n_sites, quality }], summary: { n_segments, total_bp, longest_bp, n_segments_gt_1mb } }. Add `disabled: true` once the parser stub is committed if you want the dispatcher to soft-fail.
